images.caption:
Figure 1:VGF: Value Gradient Flow.
VGF reframes behavior-regularized RL as an optimal transport from the behavior distribution towards the Boltzmann value distribution, with the transport budget as implicit regularization. This scales to large generative models and enables adaptive test-time scaling.
Figure 2:Toycase results. VGF generates actions with higher ground-truth reward than other methods.

Figure 3:OGBench offline-to-online RL results.Learning curves for online fine-tuning of VGF and FQL across all default tasks. VGF not only provides a stronger initialization from offline training but also leads to faster adaptation and higher final success rates. The shaded gray area denotes offline training.
Figure 4:Ablation study on VGF train-time flow stepsLtrainL_{\textrm{train}}.
Figure 5:VGF enables adaptive test-time scaling behavior by adjusting test-time flow stepsLtestL_{\textrm{test}}.
